research_tasks.task_title: Replot the cleaned compressibility map and summary panels from the replot-ready workbook
research_tasks.task_description: Use the replot-ready Excel workbook to reproduce the figure(s) encoded in the file. First inspect the workbook structure and identify each sheet, axis labels, and any embedded summary tables. Then reconstruct the plotted data into publication-style figures using the annotated variables in the spreadsheet. If the workbook contains multiple figure panels, generate a multi-panel figure that matches the original layout as closely as possible, preserving axis labels, units, color scales, and any error bars or annotations present in the source file. For any 2D map sheets, render the data as a heatmap or contour plot with the correct axes and colorbar. For any extracted-gap or parameter-sweep sheets, plot the dependent variable versus the independent variable with error bars where provided. Ensure the final output is a faithful replot from the cleaned data rather than a re-analysis of raw measurements.
execution_steps: Step_0: Open the workbook and enumerate all sheets, noting which figure panel each sheet corresponds to and whether it contains a 2D map, a summary table, or a parameter sweep.
Step_1: Parse the header rows/columns carefully to reconstruct the x-axis and y-axis variables, units, and any grouped columns or repeated panel blocks.

Step_2: For each sheet, convert the tabular values into the appropriate plot type: heatmap/contour for map-like data, line plot with error bars for extracted quantities, or scatter/line plot for model-sweep data.
Step_3: Match the visual styling to a Nature-style figure: clear axis labels, readable tick marks, consistent color mapping, and panel lettering if multiple subpanels are present.
Step_4: If the workbook includes multiple panels in one sheet, assemble them into a single composite figure in the same order as the source workbook or sheet labeling.
Step_5: Export the replot as a publication-quality figure and provide a short note describing any ambiguities in axis reconstruction or panel mapping.

relevant_variables: Any x-axis variable names and units
Any y-axis variable names and units
Any summary-table parameters such as B (T), Δμ (meV), w0/w1, σ(F)

ground_truth_outcome.description: A publication-style replot of the cleaned source-data workbook, reproducing the figure(s) encoded in the file with correct axes, units, and visual encoding.
